name: Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit - Heat Pumps
source: Internal mock based on IRS 25C guidance
eligibility: Owner-occupied existing homes may qualify for 30% of qualified heat pump costs up to the annual heat pump cap.
citation_snippet: The federal 25C credit can cover 30% of qualifying heat pump costs up to a $2,000 annual limit.

name: Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit - Insulation and Air Sealing
source: Internal mock based on IRS 25C guidance
eligibility: Existing homes may qualify for 30% of qualified insulation and air sealing material costs, subject to the annual envelope cap.
citation_snippet: The federal 25C credit can cover 30% of qualifying building envelope improvements, including insulation and air sealing, up to the annual cap.

name: Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit - Heat Pump Water Heaters
source: Internal mock based on IRS 25C guidance
eligibility: Existing homes may qualify for 30% of qualified heat pump water heater costs up to the annual heat pump equipment cap.
citation_snippet: Heat pump water heaters are included in the 25C high-efficiency heat pump equipment category with a $2,000 annual cap.

name: Georgia Power Home Energy Improvement Rebate - Heat Pump
eligibility: Modeled as available to Georgia Power residential customers installing qualifying high-efficiency heat pump equipment.
citation_snippet: Georgia Power residential efficiency programs commonly include rebates for qualifying HVAC upgrades.

name: Georgia Power Home Energy Improvement Rebate - Envelope
eligibility: Modeled as available to Georgia Power residential customers completing qualifying insulation or air sealing work.
citation_snippet: Georgia Power residential efficiency programs commonly include rebates for qualifying home envelope improvements.

name: Atlanta Area Weatherization Assistance
eligibility: Modeled as potentially available for income-qualified households in the Atlanta area. Household income verification is required.
citation_snippet: Georgia's Weatherization Assistance Program supports energy efficiency improvements for eligible low-income households.
